title: Create a Traefik IngressRoute
description:
## Create a Traefik IngressRoute

An `IngressRoute` is Traefik's native CRD for routing HTTP traffic to a backend Service. It replaces the standard Kubernetes `Ingress` with a richer, more expressive syntax.

**Your task:**

A Service named `web-app` is running in the `traefik-lab` namespace on port `80`. Create an `IngressRoute` named `web-app-route` in the same namespace that:
- Listens on the `web` EntryPoint
- Routes all requests matching `PathPrefix(\`/app\`)` to the `web-app` Service on port `80`

```bash
# Verify:
kubectl get ingressroute web-app-route -n traefik-lab -o jsonpath='{.spec.routes[0].match}'
```

hints.title: IngressRoute structure
hints.body: An IngressRoute references an EntryPoint by name and defines one or more routes with a match rule and a list of backend services.
hints.command:
kubectl apply -f - <<'EOF'
apiVersion: traefik.io/v1alpha1
kind: IngressRoute
metadata:
  name: web-app-route
  namespace: traefik-lab
spec:
  entryPoints:
  - web
  routes:
  - match: PathPrefix(`/app`)
    kind: Rule
    services:
    - name: web-app
      port: 80
EOF

setup_commands.command: kubectl create deployment web-app --image=nginx:alpine -n traefik-lab 2>/dev/null || true
kubectl expose deployment web-app --port=80 -n traefik-lab 2>/dev/null || true

validation.description: Check that IngressRoute web-app-route exists with the correct rule and backend

commands.command: kubectl get ingressroute web-app-route -n traefik-lab -o jsonpath='{.metadata.name}'
kubectl get ingressroute web-app-route -n traefik-lab -o jsonpath='{.spec.entryPoints[0]}'
kubectl get ingressroute web-app-route -n traefik-lab -o jsonpath='{.spec.routes[0].match}'

commands.description: Backend service is web-app on port 80
